cpuUsage.description: How hard your instance's processor is working. Sustained high usage slows down API requests and background jobs.
cpuUsage.title: CPU Usage

diskUsed.description: How much of your instance's storage the database, files, and logs are using. A full disk stops writes and can take the backend offline.
diskUsed.title: Disk Usage

memoryUsage.description: How much of your instance's RAM is in use. When memory runs low, processes can be killed or start swapping, which hurts performance.
memoryUsage.title: Memory Usage

networkIn.description: Rate of data flowing into your instance, such as file uploads and incoming API requests.
networkIn.title: Network In

networkOut.description: Rate of data leaving your instance, such as query results and file downloads served to clients.
networkOut.title: Network Out

live.step1Description: Log in to the Razorpay Dashboard.
live.step2Description: Use the Live Mode toggle. If keys are unavailable, add website details first.
live.step3Description: Under Website and app settings, generate the keys and save both values.
live.step3Title: Go to Account & Settings -> API Keys
live.step4Description: Click the button below to open Payments Settings, then paste the Key ID and Key Secret.

test.step1Description: Log in to the Razorpay Dashboard.
test.step2Description: Use the Test Mode toggle before generating test API keys.
test.step3Description: Under Website and app settings, click Generate Key and save both values.
test.step3Title: Go to Account & Settings -> API Keys
test.step4Description: Click the button below to open Payments Settings, then paste the Key ID and Key Secret.

live.step1Description: Log in to the Stripe Dashboard.
live.step2Description: Use the live account or mode that should process production payments.
live.step2Title: Make sure live mode is selected
live.step3Description: Under Standard keys, copy an sk_live_ key. New secret keys are shown once.
live.step4Description: Click the button below to open Payments Settings, then paste the secret key.

test.step1Description: Log in to the Stripe Dashboard.
test.step2Description: Turn on View test data, or choose the Sandbox that should own this key.
test.step2Title: Make sure you're in test/sandbox mode
test.step3Description: Under Standard keys, copy a secret key that starts with sk_test_.
test.step4Description: Click the button below to open Payments Settings, then paste the secret key.

payments.razorpayKeyIdHintBefore: Use a Razorpay Key ID that starts with

payments.razorpayWebhookStep5: Save the webhook, then make a test payment to verify delivery.
payments.razorpayWebhooksIntro: Razorpay webhooks must be added manually in the Razorpay Dashboard.

payments.subscriptionItemsDescription: Items associated with this subscription, including product and price links.
payments.subscriptionItemsHint: Provider items will appear after the subscription webhook projection is updated.
payments.syncDescriptionRazorpay: Force a manual sync of Razorpay items, plans, customers, subscriptions, invoices, and payments. Normally, Razorpay events sync automatically via webhooks.
payments.syncDescriptionStripe: Force a manual sync of Stripe products, prices, customers, and active subscriptions. Normally, Stripe events sync automatically via webhooks.
payments.tryDifferentProductSearch: Try a different product name, ID, or pricing reference.
payments.tryDifferentSubscriptionSearch: Try a different subscription, customer, invoice, or product reference.
payments.tryDifferentTransactionSearch: Try a different payment type, customer, payment intent, or invoice reference.
payments.webhookNeedsKey: Webhook setup uses the saved Stripe API key, so the key must be configured first.
